_comment: Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/Vaginal Myomectomy/ : Vaginal Myomectomy_Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + Vaginal Myomectomy_PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists).
clinical_indications: Submucous fibroid protruding through the external Os with an inaccessible pedicle

preauth.label: Clinical notes clearly indicating the reason(s) for admission, symptoms, signs, physical examination and procedure to be performed
Lab investigations (Complete Blood count, Hemoglobin, Thyroid function test)
USG Abdomen/ pelvis confirming presence of uterine fibroid/ polyp and its size

claim.label: Detailed indoor case papers clearly indicating the need for performing the surgery
Discharge summary with follow up advice
Histopathology form confirming submission of specimen removed for histopathological examination

ppd_preauth.q: Was the clinical presentation, physical examination & supporting investigations indicative of procedure?

cpd_claim.q: Does the patient have a definitive indication for performing this surgery?
Are detailed operative notes available with indications for and outcomes of the procedure?
Is discharge summary available with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?
Is the picture(s) of gross specimen removed date, timed with patient ID available?
Is the Histopathology form confirming submission of specimen removed for histopathological examination available?

common_queries: Clinical presentation, physical examination, or supporting investigations not indicative of the procedure.
USG Abdomen/pelvis report missing or not confirming presence/size of uterine fibroid/polyp at pre-authorization.
Detailed operative notes missing or not clearly detailing indications and outcomes for the procedure at claim submission.
Picture(s) of gross specimen removed missing or not correctly date/time stamped with patient ID.
Histopathology form confirming submission of specimen not provided at claim submission.

clinical_key_pointers: Vaginal myomectomy is a minimally invasive surgery as an alternative to abdominal or laparoscopic myomectomy.
The diagnosis made should be backed by clinical &/or USG findings and all required reports shall be uploaded.
